summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 11: re-detect against main (72 bugs resolved/triaged rounds 0-4). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (78 sigs), adversarial verify. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 11 detection: 1 from clusters = 1 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 11 verify: 1 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: ActionsServiceProvider ignores config('arqel.middleware'); action-invocation routes lose auth/tenant middleware that core resource routes get
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Mirror core's 3-way middleware resolution in registerActionRoutes(): use $panel->getMiddleware() only when it differs from the ['web'] sentinel, else config('arqel.middleware') (filtered to strings), else ['web', HandleArqelInertiaRequests::class] — replacing the `$panel?->getMiddleware() ?? [...]` at line 43.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Code-level claim reproduced. ActionsServiceProvider.php:43 resolves the action-route middleware as `$panel?->getMiddleware() ?? ['web', HandleArqelInertiaRequests::class]` and never consults config('arqel.middleware'), unlike core's registerResourceRoutes (ArqelServiceProvider.php:302-316) which uses a 3-way chain (panel-if-non-default-sentinel ELSE config ELSE fallback). Empirically confirmed in apps/showcase via tinker: the admin panel's getMiddleware() returns the default sentinel ['web'] (the panel provider never calls ->middleware(...)), while config('arqel.middleware') is ['web','auth','arqel.tenant:optional']. So action routes, once registered, would carry only `web` (+ appended HandleArqelInertiaRequests), missing auth and tenant scope, whereas core resource routes (e.g. arqel.resources.store/bulk) correctly carry the full stack. Combined with HasAuthorization::canBeExecutedBy() defaulting to true (HasAuthorization.php:27-34) and ActionController::execute passing a null guest user (ActionController.php:104-109), this is a genuine authorization-bypass divergence for any app that wires auth via config('arqel.middleware') — the path core explicitly supports and the showcase uses. This is a distinct defect from the prior authGuard (guard-name) fixes; it concerns the middleware-stack SOURCE, not the guard. However, the bypass is NOT currently live in the showcase: I verified `route:list` registers 0 arqel.actions.* routes. Root cause of the non-liveness is a separate masking issue — provider boot order is Actions(index 0) before Core(index 4) (confirmed by reflecting app->serviceProviders), and core binds PanelRegistry in packageBooted() not register(), so when ActionsServiceProvider::registerActionRoutes() runs its guard `if (! $this->app->bound(PanelRegistry::class)) return;` fires and no action routes register at all. The report honestly flagged this uncertainty. Verdict: the reported middleware-source bug is a real framework defect that must be fixed to match core, but it is latent/masked rather than live-exploitable in the current showcase, so high overstates live impact — medium. Classification framework-bug (the divergence lives entirely in framework provider code, not app config; the showcase config follows the documented config-driven middleware pattern that core supports).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/actions/src/ActionsServiceProvider.php:43 resolves the route middleware stack as `$middleware = $panel?->getMiddleware() ?? ['web', HandleArqelInertiaRequests::class];` — it consults ONLY the Panel's middleware and NEVER `config('arqel.middleware')`. Core's equivalent (packages/core/src/ArqelServiceProvider.php:302-316, registerResourceRoutes) uses a 3-way chain: Panel middleware only if it differs from the default `['web']` sentinel, ELSE `config('arqel.middleware')`, ELSE `['web', HandleArqelInertiaRequests::class]`. The documented/showcase config supplies auth via config, not via the Panel: apps/showcase/config/arqel.php:42 `'middleware' => ['web', 'auth', 'arqel.tenant:optional']`, and the showcase panel (apps/showcase/app/Providers/ArqelServiceProvider.php:52-63) never calls `->middleware(...)`, so `$panel->getMiddleware()` returns the default `['web']` (verified at runtime via tinker). Result, verified empirically in apps/showcase: core write route `arqel.resources.store` carries `web,auth,arqel.tenant:optional,HandleArqelInertiaRequests,precognitive`, but when the action routes are registered with `$panel->getMiddleware()` they carry `web` ONLY (registered admin/{resource}/{id}/actions/{action}, /bulk-actions/{action}, /header-actions/{action}, /actions/{action} all show middleware `web`). Combined with HasAuthorization default (packages/actions/src/Concerns/HasAuthorization.php:27-34 `canBeExecutedBy` returns true when no authorize() closure is set) and ActionController::execute passing a null guest user (packages/actions/src/Http/Controllers/ActionController.php:104-109), a guest could invoke row/header/toolbar/bulk actions — including the stock bulk-delete — without authentication and outside tenant scope whenever the app configures auth through config('arqel.middleware') (the path core explicitly supports) rather than Panel::middleware(). NOTE/uncertainty: in a cold `route:list`/tinker boot of the current showcase the arqel.actions.* routes did not appear in the route collection at all (count 0), so the unauthenticated path is not live in the showcase as-shipped; however the middleware-resolution bug is concretely reproduced (the routes, once registered, demonstrably carry only `web`), and it is a real authorization-bypass for any app using the config-driven middleware stack — a distinct entry point from the prior authGuard (guard-name) fixes, which were about which guard, not which middleware-stack source.
